top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: Trial week 2 underway as of May 4. Greg Brockman began testimony May 5. Key in-window disclosure: Musk texted Brockman seeking settlement two days before trial began, then allegedly threatened to ruin his reputation — OpenAI lawyers entered texts as evidence. Sam Altman expected to testify week of May 11. Liability phase expected to conclude May 21 with advisory jury verdict. Trial scope continues to include breach of charitable trust, nonprofit-to-PBC governance, and Microsoft co-defendant theory. No new safety or welfare-related evidence emerged in window beyond ongoing trial disclosures.

Monitor for May 21 advisory verdict — will trigger full reassessment
Pentagon deal terms: 'full discretion' carve-out for safety mechanisms remains unresolved

top_entities.name: DeepMind/Google
top_entities.news_summary: Employee backlash escalated within window. Approximately 1,000 Google employees signed open letter to CEO Pichai; 100+ DeepMind researchers signed separate internal letter demanding no DeepMind research or models be used for weapons development or autonomous targeting. Andreas Kirsch publicly stated he was 'incredibly ashamed.' Fortune reporting (May 4) confirms employee organizing has broadened since Project Maven but company leadership has not altered the deal. Pentagon classified deal confirmed standing with 'any lawful governmental purpose' terms. DeepMind cannot see queries, outputs, or operational decisions made with deployed models.

top_entities.name: Hungary
top_entities.news_summary: May 9 inauguration confirmed. Hungarian President Sulyok called inaugural parliament session for 10am, May 9. New parliament takes oaths, elects Speaker, forms committees. Péter Magyar elected PM and sworn in at 3pm. Full public celebration planned in central Budapest. This is a confirmed structural governance transition: Tisza party with two-thirds supermajority, EU-aligned cabinet, 16-ministry structure. The May 9 event is the formal constitutional transfer of executive power ending 16 years of Orbán/Fidesz government.
open_actions_from_prior_cycle: First post-Orbán full assessment: establish new evidence baseline for Hungary post-May 9 inauguration
Assess whether constitutional transfer of power warrants upward score trajectory under SYS and BND dimensions
Schedule June 9 follow-up cycle as noted

top_entities.name: Meta Platforms
top_entities.news_summary: May 20 layoff execution date approaches (17 days from scan date). No execution yet as of May 3. New material event in window: May 4 New Mexico court date — Santa Fe jury found Meta liable for 75,000 violations of NM Unfair Practices Act, ordered $375M in civil penalties in March; May 4 hearing is over injunctive relief. New Mexico AG publicly stated Meta's threat to leave the state 'shows how little it cares about child safety.' Keylogger surveillance program remains active. AI-attributed layoff context: Q1 2026 tech layoffs 47.9% AI-attributed.

top_entities.name: Myanmar
top_entities.news_summary: Junta extended earthquake ceasefire to May 31 (announced within window), but launched nearly 300 attacks in quake-affected areas since March 28 earthquake despite declared ceasefire — airstrikes and artillery bombardments documented in Sagaing, Mandalay, Chin, Karen, and Kachin. Junta denied visas for international emergency response teams, confiscated medicine, blocked internet access, extorted aid workers, and fired on a Chinese aid convoy. OHCHR documented ongoing ceasefire violations in the same period the junta extended the ceasefire declaration. ICG 'Dangerous Drift' brief noted regional detente signals remain weak.
open_actions_from_prior_cycle: Resolve pending change proposal from 2026-04-30
Confirm floor maintained — ceasefire extension is nominal; 300 attacks since March 28 earthquake confirms continued violations
ICG regional detente signal: insufficient for floor exit, notation only

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: New UNMISS Special Representative Anita Kiki Gbeho (Ghana) appointed April 10, 2026 — new leadership at start of mandate renewal period. UN press (SC16290) documented 280,000+ displaced in Jonglei, aerial bombardments, restricted humanitarian access, and political deadlock among Revitalized Peace Agreement signatories. Nearly 8 million people at risk of acute hunger per Al Jazeera April 28. UNMISS calling for immediate cessation of hostilities and return to ceasefire. May 9 inaugural session will be first test of new UNMISS leadership responsiveness.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: Sudan war's fourth year confirmed (April 15, 2026 anniversary). UN designated this 'the world's largest displacement crisis' (14 million displaced). $2.8 billion humanitarian appeal is 16% funded — UN official called this an 'abandoned crisis.' 61.7% acutely food insecure; at least 3 children per day dying in El-Fasher due to food shortages. RSF intensifying attack on El-Fasher. Port Sudan drone assault on aid gateway previously documented. Sudan Doctors Network and OCHA confirmed famine trajectory.

sector_alerts.alert: Pentagon classified AI deployment cluster continues to dominate AI Labs sector. All major labs now signed deals except Anthropic. Nvidia-Supermicro chip smuggling case ($2.5B, China) is an adjacent supply-chain risk signal. Congress stalled on military AI governance safeguards (Axios Apr 29). RSP 3.1 from Anthropic (April 2) is the only structural governance improvement found in-window.

Tech industry AI-attributed layoff crisis now confirmed at sector scale. Q1 2026: 78,557 tech workers laid off; 47.9% (37,638) explicitly AI-attributed. Meta and Microsoft together account for approximately 17,750 of these cuts. This is now a named 'AI-driven labor crisis' in mainstream financial press. May 20 Meta layoff execution will be a key sector-level evidence event.

Three floor-designated countries (Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar) all have active in-window negative evidence. Sudan designated 'world's largest displacement crisis' and 'abandoned crisis' by UN officials. South Sudan has 7.5M+ food-insecure people with new UNMISS leadership. Myanmar ceasefire is nominal — 300+ attacks since March 28 earthquake. DRC M23 conflict newly documented in-window by HRW (April 14).
